summary.intent: Migrate Pipelines-as-Code authentication from GitHub PAT to GitHub App credentials on OpenShift/Konflux cluster. The challenge was that GitHub App tokens expire after 1 hour while Tekton git-clone tasks require basic-auth credentials.
summary.outcome: Implemented a solution where doozer generates a fresh GitHub App installation token per invocation, creates a uniquely-named transient secret (art-transient-pipeline-auth-<nanosecond-epoch>), wires it into all PipelineRuns in the batch, deletes it after completion, and cleans up stale secrets from previous runs (>24h old). PR #2711 created.

repo: art-tools uses doozer/pyartcd to manually trigger Tekton PipelineRuns with PAC explicitly disabled
PipelineRun template has a parameterized git_auth_secret variable defaulting to 'pipelines-as-code-secret'
Only clone-repository and prefetch-dependencies tasks need git credentials; all subsequent tasks use OCI artifacts
Codebase already uses time.time_ns() for unique doozer-watch-id labels
Existing github_auth.py module provides get_github_app_token_for_org() with caching

code.finding: GitHub App private keys cannot be used directly by git - they require a two-step JWT exchange via GitHub API to obtain a usable installation token
Cleanup method uses cutoff timestamp to filter secrets by age; requires separate deletion for current run's own secret
start_pipeline_run_for_image_build already accepts git_auth_secret parameter with default 'pipelines-as-code-secret'

workflow: PAC's secret-auto-create feature only works for webhook-triggered PipelineRuns, not manually created ones
Using nanosecond-precision timestamps (time.time_ns()) eliminates collision risk for concurrent processes
404 errors during secret deletion should be ignored for idempotent cleanup
Cleanup operations should be wrapped in try/finally blocks to ensure execution even when builds fail
Kubernetes V1Secret.data expects base64-encoded values; stringData accepts plaintext
Tekton kubernetes.io/basic-auth with username 'x-access-token' is the standard pattern for GitHub App tokens

friction: Initial confusion about whether to use PAC controller's secret-auto-create vs. having doozer manage tokens directly
Separate deployment issue discovered: GITHUB_APP_INSTALLATION_ID not set in environment, causing multi-installation auto-detection failures in build-layered-products job
CodeRabbit review required multiple iterations to address instance attribute issues and cleanup robustness
Pre-existing test failures on macOS (/var vs /private/var symlink issues) unrelated to changes
AsyncMock patching issues in test_images_konflux.py when adding new async method calls
open_items: GITHUB_APP_INSTALLATION_ID needs to be set in artcd-task environment to fix multi-installation auto-detection errors (affects build-layered-products job #1806)
Pre-existing test failures in test_util.py (macOS path symlink issues) remain unfixed
User requested inline comments throughout PR but only nanosecond epoch comment was added before conversation ended
PR #2711 created but not yet merged
